core_mechanism: Spectral embedding using the k lowest eigenvectors of a Laplacian (diffusion) operator, paired with a two-sided isoperimetric bound that ties the k-th smallest eigenvalue to the best partition of the medium into k weakly-connected pieces (a higher-order Cheeger / eigenvalue-gap inequality)
brief_reason: The abstract equates k near-zero Laplacian eigenvalues with a partition into k sparse-cut subsets, justifies embedding vertices via the bottom k eigenvectors into R^k, and proves an expansion bound of O(sqrt(lambda_k log k)) governed by the k-th eigenvalue.
